answer: OpenAI was founded in 2015 as a nonprofit research lab, and later transitioned to a for-profit subsidiary in 2019. In 2025, it restructured into a public benefit corporation.

results.snippet: *   [2015: Founding and nonprofit years](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref370025). *   [2022–2023: Spearheading the AI renaissance](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref370028). *   [Leadership turmoil and restructuring](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref452733). *   [2024: Partnerships, product launches, and departures](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref400902). *   [2025: Governance, restructuring, and expansion](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref415779). *   [Transition to a public benefit corporation](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref453833). *   [Partnership with AMD](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref453834). *   [Atlas browser](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref452734). *   [IO acquisition and ChatGPT-4o launch](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref450150). *   [ChatGPT-5 launch and oversight concerns](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref450152). *   [2015: Founding and nonprofit years](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref370025). *   [2022–2023: Spearheading the AI renaissance](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref370028). *   [Leadership turmoil and restructuring](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref452733). *   [2024: Partnerships, product launches, and departures](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref400902). *   [2025: Governance, restructuring, and expansion](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref415779). *   [Transition to a public benefit corporation](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref453833). *   [Partnership with AMD](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref453834). *   [Atlas browser](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref452734). *   [IO acquisition and ChatGPT-4o launch](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref450150). *   [ChatGPT-5 launch and oversight concerns](https://www.britannica.com/money/OpenAI#ref450152). In 2024, OpenAI partnered with [Arizona State University](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Arizona-State-University), [Reddit,](https://www.britannica.com/money/Reddit)[News Corp](https://www.britannica.com/money/News-Corporation), and [Apple](https://www.britannica.com/money/Apple-Inc), acquired Multi and Chat.com, and released SearchGPT, GPT-4, OpenAI o1, and ChatGPT Pro. OpenAI is an American [artificial intelligence](https://www.britannica.com/technology/artificial-intelligence) company known for developing ChatGPT, DALL-E, and Whisper, tools that helped spark the global boom in generative AI.

results.title: OpenAI began decade ago as nonprofit lab. Musk and Altman now rivals
results.snippet: # Altman and Musk launched OpenAI as a nonprofit 10 years ago. Far from the nonprofit realm, OpenAI has emerged as one of the fastest-growing commercial entities on the planet, zooming to a $500 billion private market valuation, with almost all of that value accruing since the company's launch of ChatGPT three years ago. OpenAI and xAI are two of the main companies, along with Google, Anthropic and Meta, pouring money into AI models, as the market rapidly evolves from text-based chatbots to AI-generated videos and more advanced compute-intensive forms of content, as well as into agentic AI, with large enterprises customizing tools to enhance productivity. In February of the following year, Musk left the OpenAI board, and said at the time the move was to avoid a potential conflict of interest as his car company, Tesla, dove deeper into AI. Musk sued OpenAI and Altman in early 2024, alleging they abandoned the company's founding mission to develop AI "for the benefit of humanity broadly," and he's regularly criticized OpenAI's close ties to Microsoft, its principal backer.
